      stop("Error: You must provide either 'month' or 'quarter'.")
    }
    if(!is.character(company)){
      stop("Error: Company must be a string.")
    }
    if(!is.null(month) && !is.character(month)){
      stop("Error: Month must be a string.")
    }
    if(!is.null(quarter) && (!is.numeric(quarter)||!(quarter %in% 1:4))){
      stop("Error: Quarter must be a number between 1 and 4.")
    }
    if(!is.numeric(year)||year<=0||year!=as.integer(year)){
      stop("Error: The year must be a positive nonzero integer.")
    }
    #Status message
    if(!is.null(month)){
      message("Running model for company: ",company," | Month: ",month," ",year)
    } else{
      message("Running model for company: ",company," | Quarter: Q",quarter," ",year)
    }
    #Locate files
    folder_path=paste0(directory,"Programs/Missouri Works/",company,"/Payroll")
    all_files=list.files(path=folder_path,pattern="\\.xls[x]?$",full.names=TRUE)
    rename_files(all_files)
    all_files=list.files(path=folder_path,pattern="\\.xls[x]?$",full.names=TRUE)
    all_files_lower=tolower(all_files)
    #Initialize list of month variants
    month_variants=character(0)
    if(!is.null(month)){
      #Add variants for single month
      month_num=match(tolower(month),tolower(month.name))
      month_variants=tolower(c(month,substr(month,1,3),sprintf("%02d",month_num),as.character(month_num)))
      #Determine quarter of this month
      quarter_from_month=ceiling(month_num/3)
    }
    if(!is.null(quarter)){
      #Expand quarter months and generate variants
      quarter_months_list=list(c("January","February","March"),c("April","May","June"),c("July","August","September"),
                               c("October","November","December"))
      quarter_months=quarter_months_list[[as.integer(quarter)]]
      for(m in quarter_months){
        m_num=match(tolower(m),tolower(month.name))
        month_variants=c(month_variants,tolower(c(m,substr(m,1,3),sprintf("%02d",m_num),as.character(m_num))))
      }
    }
    #Remove duplicates
    month_variants=unique(month_variants)
    #Match any file that includes the year and at least one month variant
    if(tolower(company)!="demo"){
      file_matches=all_files[sapply(seq_along(all_files_lower),function(i){
        fname=all_files_lower[i]
        has_year=grepl(paste0("\\b",year,"\\b"),fname)
        has_month=any(sapply(month_variants,function(mv){
          grepl(paste0("(^|[^[:alnum:]])",mv,"([^[:alnum:]]|$)"),fname)
        }))
        has_year && has_month
      })]
      #If no matches, try matching quarter keywords
      if(length(file_matches)==0 && !is.null(quarter)){
        quarter_keywords=c("quarter","qtr",paste0("q",quarter))
        file_matches=all_files[sapply(all_files_lower,function(fname){
          grepl(year,fname) && any(sapply(quarter_keywords,function(qk) grepl(qk,fname)))
        })]
      }
      #Also allow YYYY.QX formatted files
      if(length(file_matches)==0){
        if(!is.null(month)){
          #Get quarter from month
          q=quarter_from_month
          pattern=paste0("\\b",year,"\\.q",q,"\\b")
          file_matches=all_files[grepl(pattern,all_files_lower)]
        } else if(!is.null(quarter)){
          pattern=paste0("\\b",year,"\\.q",quarter,"\\b")
          file_matches=all_files[grepl(pattern,all_files_lower)]
        }
      }
      #Import matched files
      if(length(file_matches)==0){
        warning("No matching files found.")
      } else{
        for(file_path in file_matches){
          df_name=paste0("df_",gsub("[^[:alnum:]]","_",tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file_path))))
          assign(df_name,readxl::read_excel(file_path))
          print(paste("Imported:",df_name))
        }
      }
      #List all objects in the environment matching the pattern
      all_objects=ls(pattern="^df_")
      #Filter only objects that are data frames
      df_list=all_objects[sapply(all_objects,function(x) is.data.frame(get(x)))]
      clean_and_transform_new=function(df){  
        #Convert everything to character first
        df=df%>%mutate(across(everything(),as.character))
        #Remove mostly-empty columns
        n_rows_to_check=min(10,nrow(df))
        keep_cols=sapply(df,function(col){
          first_rows=col[1:n_rows_to_check]
          majority_na=mean(is.na(col)|col=="")<=0.95
          first_rows_nonempty=any(!is.na(first_rows) & first_rows!="")
          majority_na && first_rows_nonempty
        })
        df=df[,keep_cols,drop=FALSE]
        #Shift header if needed
        is_numbered_format=function(string) grepl("^\\.\\.\\..*\\d+$",string)
        column_names=colnames(df)
        if(sum(sapply(column_names,is_numbered_format))>=length(column_names)/2){
          full_rows=which(rowSums(!is.na(df) & df!="")==ncol(df))
          first_full_row=if(length(full_rows)>0) full_rows[1] else NA
          if(!is.na(first_full_row)){
            colnames(df)=as.character(df[first_full_row,])
            df=df[(first_full_row+1):nrow(df),]
          }
        }
        #Clean column names
        df=df%>%clean_names()
        #Trim to last majority-full row
        majority_full_rows=which(rowSums(!is.na(df) & df!="")>=ncol(df)/2)
        if(length(majority_full_rows)>0) df=df[1:max(majority_full_rows),]
        #Safe date parser
        safe_parse_date=function(x){
          x=trimws(as.character(x))
          x[x %in% c("","NA","N/A","NULL")]=NA
          #Only parse strings that look like dates (digits + / or -)
          looks_like_date=grepl(".*\\d+.*[-/].*\\d+.*",x)
          res=rep(NA,length(x))
          if(any(looks_like_date,na.rm=TRUE)){
            res[looks_like_date]=suppressWarnings(
              tryCatch(parse_date_time(x[looks_like_date],orders=c("mdy","dmy","ymd"),quiet=TRUE,tz="UTC"),
                      error=function(e) rep(NA,sum(looks_like_date))))
          }
          as.Date(res)
        }
        #Numeric conversion
        df=df%>%mutate(across(everything(),~{
          x=gsub("[$,]","",.x)
          if(all(is.na(x)|grepl("^[-]?\\d+(\\.\\d+)?$",x))) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x)) else .x
        }))
        #Date conversion
        df=df%>%mutate(across(where(is.character),~{
          parsed=safe_parse_date(.x)
          if(mean(!is.na(parsed))>0.5) parsed else .x
        }))
        return(df)
      }
      clean_and_transform=function(df){
        #Function to check if a string is in the format "...NUMBER"
        is_numbered_format=function(string){
          grepl("^\\.\\.\\..*\\d+$",string)  #Match columns starting with "..." followed by digits
        }
        #Get the column names of the data frame
        column_names=colnames(df)
        #Check if majority of the column titles are in the format "...NUMBER"
        if(sum(sapply(column_names,is_numbered_format))>=length(column_names)/2){
          #If majority are in the desired format, process the data frame
          #Identify the first full row with all non-NA values
          first_full_row=which(apply(df,1,function(row) all(!is.na(row))))[1]
          if(length(first_full_row)==0){
            return(df) #Return original if no full row exists
          }
          #Extract the contents of this row to use as new column names
          new_column_names=as.character(df[first_full_row,])
          #Remove rows up to and including this identified row
          cleaned_df=df[(first_full_row+1):nrow(df),]
          #Apply the new column names
          colnames(cleaned_df)=new_column_names
        } else{
          #If majority are not in the desired format, return the data frame as is
          cleaned_df=df
        }
        #Clean column names using janitor (if needed)
        cleaned_df=cleaned_df%>%clean_names()
        #Function to check if a row is majority full (at least half non-NA values)
        majority_full_row=function(row){
          sum(!is.na(row))>=length(row)/2
        }
        #Find the last majority full row
        majority_full_rows=which(apply(cleaned_df,1,majority_full_row))
        if(length(majority_full_rows)>0){
          last_majority_full_row=max(majority_full_rows)
          cleaned_df=cleaned_df[1:last_majority_full_row,]
        }
        #Convert numeric columns
        for(col_name in colnames(cleaned_df)){
          if(all(grepl("^\\d+(\\.\\d+)?$",cleaned_df[[col_name]]))){
            cleaned_df[[col_name]]=as.numeric(cleaned_df[[col_name]])
          }
        }
        return(cleaned_df)
      }
      #Apply the cleaning function to each data frame and reassign it
      for(df_name in df_list){
        cleaned_data=clean_and_transform(get(df_name))
        #Reassign the cleaned data frame back to its original name
        assign(df_name,cleaned_data)
        #Print confirmation
        print(paste("Cleaned and transformed:",df_name))
      }
      column_renaming_payroll(df_list=df_list)
      for(df_name in df_list){
        df=get(df_name)
        #Normalize and convert dates in one go
        rename_and_convert=function(df,pattern,new_name){
          idx=grep(pattern,colnames(df),ignore.case=TRUE)
          if(length(idx)>0){
            colnames(df)[idx[1]]=new_name
            df[[new_name]]=convert_to_date(df[[new_name]])
          }
          return(df)
        }
        df=rename_and_convert(df,"hire.*date|date.*hire","Hire Date")
        df=rename_and_convert(df,"rehire.*date|date.*rehire","Rehire Date")
        df=rename_and_convert(df,"term.*date|date.*term","Termination Date")
        df=rename_and_convert(df,"termination.*date|date.*termination","Termination Date")
        #Clear Termination Date if earlier than Rehire Date
        if(all(c("Termination Date","Rehire Date") %in% names(df))){
          idx=!is.na(df$`Termination Date`) & !is.na(df$`Rehire Date`) & df$`Termination Date`<df$`Rehire Date`
          df$`Termination Date`[idx]=as.Date(NA)
        }
        #Save back to environment
        assign(df_name,df)
      }
      #List of known numeric columns
      numeric_cols=c("Total Hours Worked","Gross Wages","Pay Rate","Work State Wages","Work State SIT","Days in Pay Period")
      standardize_employee_ids=function(old_data,new_data){
        generate_unique_id=function(existing_ids){
          repeat{
            candidate=paste0("X",sprintf("%06d",sample(0:999999,1)))
            if(!(candidate %in% existing_ids)) return(candidate)
          }
        }
        #Check if any Employee IDs match between datasets
        common_ids=intersect(old_data$`Employee ID`,new_data$`Employee ID`)
        #Only create New Employee ID if NO Employee IDs match
        if(length(common_ids)==0){
          #Ensure New Employee ID column exists
          if(!"New Employee ID" %in% names(old_data)) old_data$`New Employee ID`=NA_character_
          if(!"New Employee ID" %in% names(new_data)) new_data$`New Employee ID`=NA_character_
          #Create simplified name keys
          old_data=old_data%>%mutate(first_simple=tolower(sub(" .*","",`First Name`)),last_simple=tolower(`Last Name`))
          new_data=new_data%>%mutate(first_simple=tolower(sub(" .*","",`First Name`)),last_simple=tolower(`Last Name`))
          #Combine datasets
          combined=bind_rows(old_data%>%mutate(source="old"),new_data%>%mutate(source="new"))
          #Track existing New Employee IDs
          existing_ids=unique(na.omit(combined$`New Employee ID`))
          existing_ids=existing_ids[grepl("^X\\d{6}$",existing_ids)]
          #Assign New Employee IDs by person (name match)
          combined=combined%>%group_by(first_simple,last_simple)%>%mutate(`New Employee ID`={
            shared_id=unique(na.omit(`New Employee ID`))[1]
            if(is.na(shared_id)){
              shared_id=generate_unique_id(existing_ids)
              existing_ids<<-c(existing_ids,shared_id)
            }
            rep(shared_id,n())
          })%>%ungroup()%>%select(-first_simple,-last_simple)
          #Split back
          old_data=combined%>%filter(source=="old")%>%select(-source)
          new_data=combined%>%filter(source=="new")%>%select(-source)
          #Final safety: fill any remaining NA New Employee IDs
          existing_ids=unique(c(old_data$`New Employee ID`,new_data$`New Employee ID`))
          existing_ids=existing_ids[grepl("^X\\d{6}$",existing_ids)]
          for(df in list(old_data,new_data)){
            na_idx=which(is.na(df$`New Employee ID`))
            if(length(na_idx)>0){
              for(i in na_idx){
                new_id=generate_unique_id(existing_ids)
                df$`New Employee ID`[i]=new_id
                existing_ids=c(existing_ids,new_id)
              }
            }
          }
        }
        return(list(new_data=new_data,old_data=old_data))
      }
      #Function to preprocess each data frame
      preprocess_df=function(df){
        for(col in intersect(numeric_cols,colnames(df))){
          df[[col]]=suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(df[[col]])))
        }
        if("Pay Date" %in% colnames(df)){
          df$`Pay Date`=suppressWarnings(as.Date(df$`Pay Date`))
        }
        if("Employee ID" %in% colnames(df)){
          df$`Employee ID`=str_replace(as.character(df$`Employee ID`),"^0+","")
        }
        return(df)
      }
      #Helper to extract normalized key (number groups)
      extract_numeric_key=function(name){
        key=str_extract_all(name,"\\d+")[[1]]
        if(length(key)>0) paste(key,collapse="_") else NA
      }
      #Standardize column types across all data frames
      standardize_types=function(df){
        #Convert all non-Date columns to character
        df[]=lapply(df,function(col){
          if(inherits(col,"Date")){
            return(col)
          } else{
            return(as.character(col))
          }
        })
        return(df)
      }
      #Check for payroll and census
      has_payroll=any(grepl("payroll",df_list,ignore.case=TRUE))
      has_census=any(grepl("census",df_list,ignore.case=TRUE))
      if(has_payroll && has_census){
        #Map df names to numeric keys
        df_keys=data.frame(name=df_list,key=sapply(df_list,extract_numeric_key))
        payroll_dfs=df_keys%>%filter(str_detect(name,"(?i)payroll"))
        census_dfs=df_keys%>%filter(str_detect(name,"(?i)census"))
        big_df_list=list()
        #Match payroll + census by numeric key
        for(i in seq_len(nrow(payroll_dfs))){
          p_row=payroll_dfs[i,]
          match_key=p_row$key
          if(!is.na(match_key)){
            c_row=census_dfs%>%filter(key==match_key)
            if(nrow(c_row)==1){
              payroll_df=preprocess_df(get(p_row$name))
              census_df=preprocess_df(get(c_row$name))
              #Clean Employee ID
              if("Employee ID" %in% names(payroll_df)){
                payroll_df$`Employee ID`=trimws(as.character(payroll_df$`Employee ID`))
                payroll_df$`Employee ID`=sub("^0+","",payroll_df$`Employee ID`)
              }
              if("Employee ID" %in% names(census_df)){
                census_df$`Employee ID`=trimws(as.character(census_df$`Employee ID`))
                census_df$`Employee ID`=sub("^0+","",census_df$`Employee ID`)
              }
              #Drop duplicate non-ID columns
              dup_cols=intersect(setdiff(names(census_df),"Employee ID"),names(payroll_df))
              census_df=census_df%>%select(-all_of(dup_cols))
              merged_df=full_join(payroll_df,census_df,by="Employee ID")
              merged_df$PeriodKey=match_key
              big_df_list[[length(big_df_list)+1]]=merged_df
            }
          }
        }
        #Standardize types
        big_df_list=lapply(big_df_list,standardize_types)
        #Fix employee_status → character
        big_df_list=lapply(big_df_list,function(df){
          if("employee_status" %in% names(df)){
            df$employee_status=as.character(df$employee_status)
          }
          return(df)
        })
        #Logical → character
        big_df_list=lapply(big_df_list,function(df){
          logical_cols=sapply(df,is.logical)
          df[logical_cols]=lapply(df[logical_cols],as.character)
          return(df)
        })
        #Convert date columns
        date_cols=c("Hire Date","Rehire Date","Termination Date","Pay Date")
        big_df_list=lapply(big_df_list,function(df){
          for(col in intersect(date_cols,names(df))){
            df[[col]]=convert_to_date(df[[col]])
          }
          return(df)
        })
        #Combine all frames
        big_df=big_df_list[[1]]
        if(length(big_df_list)>1){
          for(i in 2:length(big_df_list)){
            next_df=big_df_list[[i]]
            standardized=standardize_employee_ids(old_data=big_df,new_data=next_df)
            big_df=bind_rows(standardized$old_data,standardized$new_data)
          }
        }
      } else{
        #Fallback processing if no payroll/census pattern
        col_titles=unique(unlist(lapply(df_list,function(name) colnames(get(name)))))
        col_titles=unique(c(col_titles,"Pay Date"))
        date_cols=c("Hire Date","Rehire Date","Termination Date","Pay Date")
        df_list_cleaned=lapply(df_list,function(df_name){
          df=get(df_name)
          #Keep only known columns
          cols_to_select=intersect(col_titles,names(df))
          df=df%>%select(all_of(cols_to_select))
          #Add missing columns
          missing_cols=setdiff(col_titles,names(df))
          for(col in missing_cols) df[[col]]=NA
          #Convert date columns
          for(col in intersect(date_cols,names(df))){
            if(!inherits(df[[col]],"Date")){
              if(inherits(df[[col]],"POSIXt")){
                df[[col]]=convert_to_date(df[[col]])
              } else if(is.logical(df[[col]])){
                df[[col]]=as.Date(NA)
              } else{
                suppressWarnings(df[[col]]<-as.Date(df[[col]]))
              }
            }
          }
          df=preprocess_df(df)
          return(df)
        })
        #Standardize types
        df_list_cleaned=lapply(df_list_cleaned,standardize_types)
        #employee_status → character
        df_list_cleaned=lapply(df_list_cleaned,function(df){
          if("employee_status" %in% names(df)){
            df$employee_status=as.character(df$employee_status)
          }
          return(df)
        })
        #logical → character
        df_list_cleaned=lapply(df_list_cleaned,function(df){
          logical_cols=sapply(df,is.logical)
          df[logical_cols]=lapply(df[logical_cols],as.character)
          return(df)
        })
        #Convert date columns again
        df_list_cleaned=lapply(df_list_cleaned,function(df){
          for(col in intersect(date_cols,names(df))){
            df[[col]]=convert_to_date(df[[col]])
          }
          return(df)
        })
        #Begin merge chain
        big_df=df_list_cleaned[[1]]
        if(length(df_list_cleaned)>1){
          for(i in 2:length(df_list_cleaned)){
            next_df=df_list_cleaned[[i]]
            standardized=standardize_employee_ids(old_data=big_df,new_data=next_df)
            big_df=bind_rows(standardized$old_data,standardized$new_data)
          }
        }
      }
    }
    if(tolower(company)=="demo"){
      message("No matching files found. Generating demo data.")
      set.seed(123)
      #Parameters
      n=100
      month_name=month
      #Helper functions
      month_to_num=function(month_name){
        match(tolower(month_name),tolower(month.name))
      }
      get_period_range=function(month_name=NULL,year=NULL,quarter=NULL){
        if(!is.null(month_name) & !is.null(year)){
          month=month_to_num(month_name)
          start_date=as.Date(paste0(year,"-",sprintf("%02d",month),"-01"))
          end_date=seq(start_date,by="month",length.out=2)[2]-1
        } else if(!is.null(quarter) & !is.null(year)){
          month_start=(quarter-1)*3+1
          start_date=as.Date(paste0(year,"-",sprintf("%02d",month_start),"-01"))
          end_month=month_start+2
          end_date=seq(as.Date(paste0(year,"-",end_month,"-01")),by="month",length.out=2)[2]-1
        } else{
          stop("Provide either month_name+year or quarter+year")
        }
        return(list(start=start_date,end=end_date))
      }
      generate_pay_dates=function(start,end){
        seq.Date(start,end,by="14 days")
      }
      terminate_employees=function(df,terminate_prob=0.05){
        is_terminated=runif(nrow(df))<terminate_prob
        if(any(is_terminated)){
          term_dates=sapply(df$`Hire Date`[is_terminated],function(h){
            sample(seq(h,max(pay_dates),by="day"),1)
          })
          df$`Termination Date`[is_terminated]=term_dates #Already Date
        }
        return(df)
      }
      add_new_hires=function(df,last_pay_date,next_pay_date,n_new=5){
        if(n_new==0) return(df)
        hire_dates=sample(seq(last_pay_date+1,next_pay_date,by="day"),n_new,replace=TRUE)
        new_ids=if(nrow(df)==0) 1001:(1000+n_new) else max(df$`Employee ID`,na.rm=TRUE)+1:n_new
        new_employees=data.frame(`Employee ID`=new_ids,`First Name`=paste0("First",new_ids),
                                  `Last Name`=paste0("Last",new_ids),`Hire Date`=hire_dates,
                                  `Termination Date`=as.Date(NA),
                                  Classification=sample(c("Full-Time","Part-Time"),n_new,replace=TRUE),
                                  `Work State`=sample(states,n_new,replace=TRUE,prob=prob_states),
                                  `Resident State`=sample(states,n_new,replace=TRUE,prob=prob_states),
                                  `Job Title`=NA,`Wage Type`=NA,`Pay Rate`=NA,`Gross Wages`=NA,`Work State Wages`=NA,
                                  `Work State SIT`=NA,`Resident State Wages`=NA,`Resident State SIT`=NA,
                                  `Total Hours Worked`=NA,`Pay Date`=NA,`Days in Pay Period`=NA,`Pay Period End Date`=NA,
                                  stringsAsFactors=FALSE,check.names=FALSE)
        #Ensure the column order and names match df
        missing_cols=setdiff(names(df),names(new_employees))
        if(length(missing_cols)>0){
          for(col in missing_cols) new_employees[[col]]=NA
        }
        new_employees=new_employees[,names(df)]
        df=rbind(df,new_employees)
        return(df)
      }
      update_employee_attributes=function(df){
        change_class=runif(nrow(df))<0.03
        df$Classification[change_class]=ifelse(df$Classification[change_class]=="Full-Time","Part-Time","Full-Time")
        change_work_state=runif(nrow(df))<0.02
        df$`Work State`[change_work_state]=sample(states,sum(change_work_state),replace=TRUE)
        change_res_state=runif(nrow(df))<0.01
        df$`Resident State`[change_res_state]=sample(states,sum(change_res_state),replace=TRUE)
        return(df)
      }
      get_work_location=function(state){
        rand=runif(1)
        if(rand<0.5) return("Headquarters")
        else if(rand<0.75) return("Regional Headquarters")
        else return("Plant")
      }
      #Initialize period & pay dates
      period=get_period_range(month_name=month_name,year=year)
      pay_dates=generate_pay_dates(period$start,period$end)
      states=state.name
      prob_states=ifelse(states=="Missouri",0.92,(1-0.92)/(length(states)-1))
      #Initialize employees
      employee_counter=1000
      hire_start_date=period$start-365*2 #2 years prior
      current_employees=data.frame(`Employee ID`=employee_counter+1:n,`First Name`=paste0("First",1:n),
                                    `Last Name`=paste0("Last",1:n),
                                    `Hire Date`=sample(seq(hire_start_date,period$end,by="day"),n,replace=TRUE),
                                    `Termination Date`=as.Date(NA),
                                    Classification=sample(c("Full-Time","Part-Time"),n,replace=TRUE),
                                    `Work State`=sample(states,n,replace=TRUE,prob=prob_states),
                                    `Resident State`=sample(states,n,replace=TRUE,prob=prob_states),
                                    `Job Title`=NA,`Wage Type`=NA,`Pay Rate`=NA,`Gross Wages`=NA,`Work State Wages`=NA,
                                    `Work State SIT`=NA,`Resident State Wages`=NA,`Resident State SIT`=NA,
                                    `Total Hours Worked`=NA,`Pay Date`=NA,`Days in Pay Period`=NA,`Pay Period End Date`=NA,
                                    check.names=FALSE)
      #Build payroll per pay date
      payroll_records=list()
      for(i in seq_along(pay_dates)){
        date=pay_dates[i]
        if(i>1){
          current_employees=terminate_employees(current_employees,0.05)
          current_employees=update_employee_attributes(current_employees)
          n_new=sample(0:5,1)
          current_employees=add_new_hires(current_employees,pay_dates[i-1],pay_dates[i],n_new)
        }
        active_employees=current_employees[is.na(current_employees$`Termination Date`)|
                                            current_employees$`Termination Date`>=date,]
        n_active=nrow(active_employees)
        if(n_active==0){
          next
        }
        classification=active_employees$Classification
        work_state=active_employees$`Work State`
        resident_state=active_employees$`Resident State`
        work_location=sapply(work_state,get_work_location)
        wage_type=sample(c("Salaried","Hourly"),n_active,replace=TRUE)
        total_hours=ifelse(classification=="Full-Time",sample(c(80,85,90),n_active,replace=TRUE),
                                                        sample(seq(20,60,5),n_active,replace=TRUE))
        pay_rate=gross_wages=numeric(n_active)
        for(j in seq_len(n_active)){
          if(wage_type[j]=="Hourly"){
            hourly_rate=runif(1,18,50)
            pay_rate[j]=round(hourly_rate,2)
            gross_wages[j]=round(hourly_rate*total_hours[j],2)
          } else{
            annual_salary=runif(1,45000,120000)
            biweekly_rate=annual_salary/26
            pay_rate[j]=round(biweekly_rate,2)
            gross_wages[j]=round(biweekly_rate,2)
          }
        }
        df=data.frame(`Employee ID`=active_employees$`Employee ID`,`First Name`=active_employees$`First Name`,
                      `Last Name`=active_employees$`Last Name`,`Hire Date`=active_employees$`Hire Date`,
                      `Termination Date`=active_employees$`Termination Date`,
                      `Employee Status`=ifelse(!is.na(active_employees$`Termination Date`) & 
                                                active_employees$`Termination Date`<date,"Terminated","Active"),
                      `Work Location`=work_location,`Job Title`="Analyst",`Classification`=classification,
                      `Wage Type`=wage_type,`Pay Rate`=pay_rate,`Gross Wages`=gross_wages,`Work State`=work_state,
                      `Work State Wages`=gross_wages,`Work State SIT`=round(gross_wages*0.05,2),
                      `Resident State`=resident_state,`Resident State Wages`=gross_wages,
                      `Resident State SIT`=round(gross_wages*0.05,2),`Total Hours Worked`=total_hours,`Pay Date`=date,
                      `Days in Pay Period`=14,`Pay Period End Date`=date,check.names=FALSE)
        payroll_records[[i]]=df
      }
      demo_df=do.call(rbind,payroll_records)
      big_df=demo_df
      message("Random demo payroll generated with terminations and new hires.")
    }
    #Function to check if a column is all NA or data frame is empty
    is_all_missing=function(df,colname){
      (colname %in% names(df)) && (all(is.na(df[[colname]]))||nrow(df)==0)
    }
    #Move "Employee ID" to the first column if it exists
    if("Employee ID" %in% names(big_df)){
      big_df=big_df[,c("Employee ID",setdiff(names(big_df),"Employee ID"))]
    }
    #Define file path
    file_path=paste0(directory,"Programs/Missouri Works/",company,"/",company," Missouri Works Report.xlsx")
    #Pre-load previous_data only if file AND "Combined Payroll Data" sheet exist
    previous_data=NULL
    if(file.exists(file_path)){
      sheet_names=names(wb)
      if("Combined Payroll Data" %in% sheet_names){
        #Get the sheet data
        tmp=read.xlsx(wb,sheet="Combined Payroll Data",colNames=TRUE,detectDates=FALSE)
        #Convert all columns to character (text)
        tmp=data.frame(lapply(tmp,as.character),stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
        #Replace periods in column names with spaces
        names(tmp)=gsub("\\."," ",names(tmp))
        #Check that not all columns are NA
        if(any(colSums(!is.na(tmp))>0)){
          #Normalize Employee ID
          tmp$`Employee ID`=gsub("^0+","",as.character(tmp$`Employee ID`))
          #Convert Pay Date
          tmp$`Pay Date`=convert_to_date(tmp$`Pay Date`)       
          #Define exclusion window
          if(!is.null(month)){
            month_num=match(tolower(month),tolower(month.name))
            start_date=as.Date(sprintf("%04d-%02d-01",year,month_num))
            end_date=ceiling_date(start_date,"month")-days(1)
          } else if(!is.null(quarter)){
            start_month=(quarter-1)*3+1
            start_date=as.Date(sprintf("%04d-%02d-01",year,start_month))
            end_date=ceiling_date(start_date %m+% months(2),"month")-days(1)
          }
          #Drop rows with missing Pay Date AND remove rows in current month/quarter window
          previous_data=tmp%>%filter(!is.na(`Pay Date`))%>%filter(!(`Pay Date`>=start_date & `Pay Date`<=end_date))
        }
      }
    }
    if("PeriodKey" %in% names(big_df)){
      big_df=big_df%>%mutate(Month=as.Date(paste0(substr(PeriodKey,1,4),"-",substr(PeriodKey,6,7),"-01")))
    } else{
      #Do nothing or return early
      message("PeriodKey column not found, skipping mutation.")
    }
    if("Position ID" %in% colnames(big_df)){
      big_df=big_df[!is.na(big_df$`Position ID`),]
    }
    #Define date parsing formats
    date_orders=c("ymd","mdy","dmy","b d Y","d-b-Y")
    #Helper: normalize a column to Date
    normalize_date_col=function(df,col){
      if(col %in% names(df)){
        non_na_vals=na.omit(df[[col]])
        if(length(non_na_vals)>0 && all(grepl("^[0-9]+$",non_na_vals))){
          #Convert numeric strings to numeric then to date
          df[[col]]=convert_to_date(as.numeric(df[[col]]))
        } else{
          #Otherwise parse with lubridate
          df[[col]]=suppressWarnings(parse_date_time(df[[col]],orders=date_orders))
          df[[col]]=as.Date(df[[col]])
        }
      }
      return(df)
    }
    #Columns to normalize
    cols_to_normalize=c("Hire Date","Rehire Date","Termination Date","Pay Date","Pay Period Start Date",
                        "Pay Period End Date","Month")
    #Loop through columns
    for(col in cols_to_normalize){
      #Apply to big_df if column exists
      if(col %in% names(big_df)){
        big_df=normalize_date_col(big_df,col)
      }
      #Apply to previous_data if it exists, is a data frame, and column exists
      if(exists("previous_data") && is.data.frame(previous_data) && col %in% names(previous_data)){
        previous_data=normalize_date_col(previous_data,col)
      }
    }
    message("Date columns normalized.")
    if(exists("previous_data") && !is.null(previous_data)){
      #Check that previous_data has "New Employee ID" and "Employee ID" columns
      if("New Employee ID" %in% colnames(previous_data) && "Employee ID" %in% colnames(previous_data)){
        #Ensure big_df has Employee ID column
        if("Employee ID" %in% colnames(big_df)){
          #Create New Employee ID column in big_df if it doesn't exist
          if(!("New Employee ID" %in% colnames(big_df))) big_df$`New Employee ID`=NA_character_
          #Fill big_df$New Employee ID by matching Employee ID
          new_employee_map=setNames(previous_data$`New Employee ID`,previous_data$`Employee ID`)
          big_df$`New Employee ID`=new_employee_map[as.character(big_df$`Employee ID`)]
          #Function to generate a unique New Employee ID like X123456
          generate_unique_id=function(existing_ids){
            repeat{
              candidate=paste0("X",sprintf("%06d",sample(0:999999,1)))
              if(!(candidate %in% existing_ids)) return(candidate)
            }
          }
          #Identify rows with missing New Employee ID
          na_rows=which(is.na(big_df$`New Employee ID`))
          if(length(na_rows)>0){
            existing_ids=big_df$`New Employee ID`[!is.na(big_df$`New Employee ID`)]
            #Assign unique IDs, updating existing_ids each time to avoid duplicates
            big_df$`New Employee ID`[na_rows]=sapply(seq_along(na_rows),function(x){
              new_id=generate_unique_id(existing_ids)
              existing_ids<<-c(existing_ids,new_id) #update global list of existing IDs
              new_id
            })
          }
        }
      }
    }
    if(exists("previous_data") && !is.null(previous_data)){
      #Standardize Employee IDs using previous_data as old_data
      standardized=standardize_employee_ids(old_data=previous_data,new_data=big_df)
      #Combine the standardized old_data and new_data
      combined_payroll_data=dplyr::bind_rows(standardized$old_data,standardized$new_data)
      if(!("New Employee ID" %in% colnames(previous_data))){
        new_employee_id_code=FALSE
      } else{
        new_employee_id_code=TRUE
      }
    } else{
      new_employee_id_code=FALSE
      #If previous_data doesn't exist, just use big_df
      combined_payroll_data=big_df
    }
    fill_cols=c("Position ID","Job Title","Last Name","First Name","Work Location","Residence State","Resident State",
                "Work State","Company","Social Security Number","Employee Status","Hire Date","Rehire Date",
                "Termination Date","Classification","Wage Type","Pay Rate")
    names(combined_payroll_data)=gsub("\\."," ",names(combined_payroll_data))
    #Keep only columns that exist
    fill_cols=intersect(fill_cols,names(combined_payroll_data))
    #Convert Pay Date to Date if not already
    if(!inherits(combined_payroll_data$`Pay Date`,"Date")){
      combined_payroll_data$`Pay Date`=as.Date(combined_payroll_data$`Pay Date`)
    }
    combined_payroll_data=combined_payroll_data%>%mutate(across(all_of(fill_cols),~{
      #Convert "NA" and "" to proper NA only for character columns
      if(is.character(.x)){
        .x=na_if(.x,"NA")
        .x=na_if(.x,"")
      }
      .x
    }))%>%arrange(`Employee ID`,`Pay Date`)%>%group_by(`Employee ID`)%>%fill(all_of(fill_cols),.direction="down")%>%ungroup()%>%distinct()
    message("Missing data filled.")
    combined_payroll_data=combined_payroll_data%>%mutate(`Hire Date`=ifelse(is.na(`Hire Date`),
                                                          as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,month_num)),`Hire Date`))
    #Ensure proper types in combined_payroll_data
    if("Pay Date" %in% colnames(combined_payroll_data)){
      combined_payroll_data$`Pay Date`=suppressWarnings(as.Date(combined_payroll_data$`Pay Date`))
    }
    if("Employee ID" %in% colnames(combined_payroll_data)){
      combined_payroll_data$`Employee ID`=str_replace(as.character(combined_payroll_data$`Employee ID`),"^0+","")
    }
    #Clean all dates first
    if("Hire Date" %in% names(combined_payroll_data)){
      combined_payroll_data$`Hire Date`=convert_to_date(combined_payroll_data$`Hire Date`)
    }
    combined_payroll_data=combined_payroll_data%>%filter(!is.na(`Pay Date`))
    cols=c("Rehire Date","Termination Date")
    for(col in intersect(cols,names(combined_payroll_data))){
      combined_payroll_data[[col]][is.na(combined_payroll_data[[col]])]=""
    }
    combined_payroll_data$`Pay Rate`=as.numeric(combined_payroll_data$`Pay Rate`)
    combined_payroll_data$`Days in Pay Period`=as.numeric(combined_payroll_data$`Days in Pay Period`)
    #If "Pay Period End Date" doesn't exist, create it from "Pay Date" - 7 days
    if(!"Pay Period End Date" %in% colnames(combined_payroll_data)){
      if(!"Pay Date" %in% colnames(combined_payroll_data)){
        stop("Missing both 'Pay Period End Date' and 'Pay Date' columns in combined_payroll_data.")
      }
      combined_payroll_data$`Pay Date`=convert_to_date(combined_payroll_data$`Pay Date`)
      combined_payroll_data$`Pay Period End Date`=combined_payroll_data$`Pay Date`-7
    }
    if("Pay.Period.End.Date" %in% colnames(combined_payroll_data)){
      colnames(combined_payroll_data)[colnames(combined_payroll_data)=="Pay.Period.End.Date"]="Pay Period End Date"
    }
    #Ensure Pay Period End Date is in Date format
    combined_payroll_data$`Pay Period End Date`=convert_to_date(combined_payroll_data$`Pay Period End Date`)
    #Base Missouri Works columns
    missouri_works_cols=c("Employee ID","Comments","Comments Old","Comments New","Last Name","First Name",
                          "Employee Status","Work Location","Job Title","Hire Date","Rehire Date","Termination Date",
                          "Classification","Wage Type","Pay Rate","Gross Wages","Work State","Work State Wages",
                          "Work State SIT","Resident State","Resident State Wages","Resident State SIT",
                          "Total Hours Worked","Sum of Days in Pay Period","Min of Pay Period Start Date",
                          "Min of Pay Period End Date","Max of Pay Period End Date","Min of Pay Date","Max of Pay Date",
                          "Count of Pay Date","MO Works Start Date","MO Works End Date","Works Days Worked")                    
    #Load baseline counts file
    baseline_path=paste0(directory,"Programs/Missouri Works/Missouri Works Baseline Counts.xlsx")
    baseline_df=read_excel(baseline_path)
    baseline_info=baseline_df%>%filter(Company==company)
    message("Baseline info imported.")
    if(exists("quarter") && !is.null(quarter)){
      if(!quarter %in% 1:4){
        stop("Quarter must be an integer between 1 and 4")
      }
      quarter_months=list(`1`=1:3,`2`=4:6,`3`=7:9,`4`=10:12)
      months_vec=quarter_months[[as.character(quarter)]]
      yy=substr(as.character(year),3,4)
      #Quarterly Work State Wages columns
      wages_cols=paste0(month.abb[months_vec],"-",yy," Work State Wages")
      wages_pos=match("Work State Wages",missouri_works_cols)
      missouri_works_cols=append(missouri_works_cols,wages_cols,after=wages_pos-1) 
      #Quarterly Work State SIT columns
      sit_cols=paste0(month.abb[months_vec],"-",yy," Work State SIT")
      sit_pos=match("Work State SIT",missouri_works_cols)
      missouri_works_cols=append(missouri_works_cols,sit_cols,after=sit_pos-1)
    }
    #Get months vector
    get_months=function(month=NULL,quarter=NULL){
      if(!is.null(month)){
        month_num=match(tolower(month),tolower(month.name))
        if(is.na(month_num)) stop("Invalid month name")
        return(month_num)
      }
      if(!is.null(quarter)){
        if(!quarter %in% 1:4) stop("quarter must be 1–4")
        return(list(`1`=1:3,`2`=4:6,`3`=7:9,`4`=10:12)[[as.character(quarter)]])
      }
      stop("Either month or quarter must be defined")
    }
    #Determine months
    months_vec=get_months(month=if(exists("month")) month else NULL,
                          quarter=if(exists("quarter")) quarter else NULL)
    yy=substr(as.character(year),3,4)
    #Build Status and Works Credit column names
    status_cols=paste0(month.abb[months_vec],"-",yy," Status")
    credit_cols=paste0(month.abb[months_vec],"-",yy," Works Credit")
    #Insert after "MO Works End Date"
    insert_pos=match("MO Works End Date",missouri_works_cols)
    if(is.na(insert_pos)){
      stop('"MO Works End Date" column not found')
    }
    #Insert Status columns
    missouri_works_cols=append(missouri_works_cols,values=status_cols,after=insert_pos)
    #Find current position of the existing column (make sure you know which column you want to keep between)
    existing_col_name="Works Days Worked"
    existing_col_pos=match(existing_col_name,missouri_works_cols)
    #Insert Credit columns after the existing column
    missouri_works_cols=append(missouri_works_cols,values=credit_cols,after=existing_col_pos)
    #Initialize empty data frame
    missouri_works=data.frame(matrix(ncol=length(missouri_works_cols),nrow=0))
    colnames(missouri_works)=missouri_works_cols
    baseline_row=baseline_info[1,]
    sd1=as.Date(baseline_row[["Start Date 1"]])
    ed1=as.Date(baseline_row[["End Date 1"]])
    sd2=as.Date(baseline_row[["Start Date 2"]])
    ed2=as.Date(baseline_row[["End Date 2"]])
    #Parse MMM-YY → Date
    parse_mmm_yy=function(col){
      m=regexec("^([A-Za-z]{3})-(\\d{2})",col)
      p=regmatches(col,m)[[1]]
      if(length(p)<3) return(NA)
      mm=match(p[2],month.abb)
      yy=as.integer(p[3])
      yyyy=ifelse(yy>=50,1900+yy,2000+yy)
      as.Date(sprintf("%04d-%02d-01",yyyy,mm))
    }
    #Is date in either window
    in_any_window=function(d){
      (!is.na(sd1) && !is.na(ed1) && d>=sd1 && d<=ed1)||(!is.na(sd2) && !is.na(ed2) && d>=sd2 && d<=ed2)
    }
    #regex (Jul-24 ...)
    target_cols=grep("^[A-Za-z]{3}-\\d{2} (Status|Works Credit)$",names(missouri_works),value=TRUE)
    #Decide which to keep
    keep_cols=sapply(target_cols,function(col){
      d=parse_mmm_yy(col)
      !is.na(d) && in_any_window(d)
    })
    #Remove out-of-range columns
    missouri_works=missouri_works%>%select(-all_of(target_cols[!keep_cols]))
    combined_payroll_data_filtered=combined_payroll_data%>%mutate(`Pay Date`=as.Date(`Pay Date`))%>%{ 
      #Conditional filtering outside filter()
      if(exists("month") && !is.null(month)){
        filter(.,year(`Pay Date`)==year & tolower(month(`Pay Date`,label=TRUE,abbr=FALSE))==tolower(month))
      } else if(exists("quarter") && !is.null(quarter)){
        filter(.,year(`Pay Date`)==year & quarter(`Pay Date`)==quarter)
      } else{
        filter(.,year(`Pay Date`)==year) #fallback if neither is defined
      }
    }
    message("Combined Payroll Data filtered to current period.")
    #Get unique Employee IDs from combined_payroll_data_filtered
    unique_ids=unique(combined_payroll_data_filtered$`Employee ID`)
    #Create a copy of missouri_works with the correct number of rows
    missouri_works=missouri_works[seq_along(unique_ids),]
    #Fill the ID column
    missouri_works$`Employee ID`=unique_ids
    #Reset row names
    rownames(missouri_works)=NULL
    missouri_works$`Employee ID`=gsub("^0+","",as.character(missouri_works$`Employee ID`))
    #Define the columns to update
    columns_to_update=c("Last Name","First Name","Hire Date","Rehire Date","Termination Date","Job Title",
                        "Employee Status","Pay Rate","Classification","Wage Type","Work Location","Work State",
                        "Resident State")
    #Loop through each row in combined_payroll_data_filtered
    for(i in seq_len(nrow(combined_payroll_data_filtered))){
      fname=trimws(tolower(combined_payroll_data_filtered$`First Name`[i]))
      lname=trimws(tolower(combined_payroll_data_filtered$`Last Name`[i]))
      #Only run this logic if Hire Date is missing
      if(is.na(combined_payroll_data_filtered$`Hire Date`[i])){
        found_valid_hire_date=FALSE
        for(df_name in df_list){
          df=get(df_name)
          #Skip if First/Last Name columns don't exist
          if(!all(c("First Name","Last Name") %in% names(df))) next
          #Normalize names in df
          df$FirstNameClean=trimws(tolower(df$`First Name`))
          df$LastNameClean=trimws(tolower(df$`Last Name`))
          #Get matching rows
          match_rows=df[df$FirstNameClean==fname & df$LastNameClean==lname,]
          #If any matching row has a valid Hire Date, stop the search
          if("Hire Date" %in% names(df) && any(!is.na(match_rows$`Hire Date`))){
            found_valid_hire_date=TRUE
            break
          }
          #If matches exist but no valid hire date, assign first-of-month based on df_name
          if(nrow(match_rows)>0){
            #Extract the date string from the df_name (match YYYY_MM or YYYY_MM_DD)
            date_match=regmatches(df_name,regexpr("\\d{4}_\\d{2}(?:_\\d{2})?",df_name))
            if(length(date_match)>0 && nzchar(date_match)){
              #If the day (_DD) is missing, append "_01" for first of month
              if(!grepl("^\\d{4}_\\d{2}_\\d{2}$",date_match)){
                date_match=paste0(date_match,"_01")
              }
              #Convert underscores to dashes for clean parsing
              date_str=gsub("_","-",date_match) #"2025_03_01" → "2025-03-01"
              #Convert to Date object
              first_of_month=as.Date(date_str,format="%Y-%m-%d")
              message("Data frame: ",df_name," → First of month: ",first_of_month)
              #Assign if parsed correctly
              if(!is.na(first_of_month)){
                combined_payroll_data_filtered$`Hire Date`[i]=first_of_month
                found_valid_hire_date=TRUE
                break
              }
            } else{
              message("No date found in df_name: ",df_name)
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
    #Update missouri_works
    for(i in seq_len(nrow(missouri_works))){
      emp_id=missouri_works$`Employee ID`[i]
      most_recent_row=combined_payroll_data_filtered%>%filter(`Employee ID`==emp_id)%>%
        arrange(desc(`Pay Date`))%>%slice(1)
      if(nrow(most_recent_row)==0 && !is.null(previous_data)){
        most_recent_row=previous_data%>%filter(`Employee ID`==emp_id)%>%
          arrange(desc(`Pay Date`))%>%slice(1)
      }
      if(nrow(most_recent_row)==1){
        for(col in columns_to_update){
          if(col %in% colnames(missouri_works) && col %in% colnames(most_recent_row) &&
              !is.na(most_recent_row[[col]])){
            missouri_works[[col]][i]=most_recent_row[[col]]
          }
        }
      }
    }
    message("Missouri Works updated.")
    missouri_works=missouri_works%>%mutate(`Hire Date`=convert_to_date(`Hire Date`),
                                            `Rehire Date`=convert_to_date(`Rehire Date`),
                                            `Termination Date`=convert_to_date(`Termination Date`))
    missouri_works=missouri_works%>%mutate(`Termination Date`=case_when(
      !is.na(`Rehire Date`) & !is.na(`Termination Date`) & `Rehire Date`>`Termination Date`~NA_Date_,
      TRUE~`Termination Date`))
    #missouri_works is now filled with the most recent available data for each Employee ID
    #Final cleanup (just in case any columns ended up as list columns)
    missouri_works[]=lapply(missouri_works,function(col){
      if(is.list(col)) sapply(col,function(x) if(length(x)>1) x[1] else x) else col
    })
    #Rename columns that end with ".X" (where X is a single letter)
    colnames(missouri_works)=gsub("\\.[A-Za-z]$","",colnames(missouri_works))
    #Set threshold for NA (e.g., 80% of columns)
    na_threshold=0.9
    #Calculate how many NAs per row
    na_fraction=rowMeans(is.na(missouri_works))
    #Keep only rows where NA fraction is below threshold
    missouri_works=missouri_works[na_fraction<=na_threshold,]
    if(exists("previous_data") && is.data.frame(previous_data) && nrow(previous_data)>0){
      #Ensure Employee ID is character
      missouri_works$`Employee ID`=as.character(missouri_works$`Employee ID`)
      previous_data$`Employee ID`=as.character(previous_data$`Employee ID`)
      #Ensure Pay Date is Date class in previous_data
      previous_data$`Pay Date`=convert_to_date(previous_data$`Pay Date`)  
      #Identify columns to fill (common columns except IDs and Pay Date)
      columns_to_fill=setdiff(intersect(names(missouri_works),names(previous_data)),c("Employee ID","Pay Date"))  
      #Align data types for date-like columns and convert others to character if needed
      for(col in columns_to_fill){
        if(grepl("Date",col,ignore.case=TRUE)){
          missouri_works[[col]]=convert_to_date(missouri_works[[col]])
          previous_data[[col]]=convert_to_date(previous_data[[col]])
        } else{
          #Convert both to character if they differ
          if(class(missouri_works[[col]])[1]!=class(previous_data[[col]])[1]){
            missouri_works[[col]]=as.character(missouri_works[[col]])
            previous_data[[col]]=as.character(previous_data[[col]])
          }
        }
      }
      #Get the most recent row per Employee ID from previous_data
      recent_rows=previous_data%>%filter(!is.na(`Pay Date`))%>%arrange(`Employee ID`,desc(`Pay Date`))%>%
                                         group_by(`Employee ID`)%>%slice(1)%>%ungroup()  
      #Left join missouri_works with the recent previous_data rows
      merged_df=missouri_works%>%left_join(recent_rows %>% select(`Employee ID`,all_of(columns_to_fill)),
                                           by="Employee ID",suffix=c("",".from_previous"))  
      #Coalesce values from previous_data into missouri_works safely with robust type alignment
      for(col in columns_to_fill){
        from_col=paste0(col,".from_previous")
        if(from_col %in% names(merged_df)){
          if(grepl("Date",col,ignore.case=TRUE)){
            #Convert both columns safely to Date
            merged_df[[col]]=convert_to_date(merged_df[[col]])
            merged_df[[from_col]]=convert_to_date(merged_df[[from_col]])  
            #Handle POSIXct / POSIXlt conversion
            if(inherits(merged_df[[col]],c("POSIXct","POSIXlt"))){
              merged_df[[col]]=as.Date(merged_df[[col]])
            }
            if(inherits(merged_df[[from_col]],c("POSIXct","POSIXlt"))){
              merged_df[[from_col]]=as.Date(merged_df[[from_col]])
            }
            #Handle logical (all NA) columns
            if(is.logical(merged_df[[col]])) merged_df[[col]]=as.Date(NA)
            if(is.logical(merged_df[[from_col]])) merged_df[[from_col]]=as.Date(NA)
          } else{
            #Non-date columns: convert both to character
            merged_df[[col]]=as.character(merged_df[[col]])
            merged_df[[from_col]]=as.character(merged_df[[from_col]])
          }
          #Now coalesce safely
          merged_df[[col]]=dplyr::coalesce(merged_df[[col]],merged_df[[from_col]])
          merged_df[[from_col]]=NULL
        }
      }
      #Assign the merged and updated dataframe back
      missouri_works=merged_df
    }
    message("Data reconciled.")
    #Apply trimws to every column if it's character or factor
    missouri_works[]=lapply(missouri_works,function(col){
      if(is.character(col)||is.factor(col)){
        trimws(as.character(col)) #convert factors to character too
      } else{
        col #leave other columns unchanged
      }
    })
    #Ensure "Hire Date" column exists and convert using convert_to_date
    if("Hire Date" %in% colnames(missouri_works)){
      missouri_works$`Hire Date`=convert_to_date(missouri_works$`Hire Date`)
    }
    #Ensure "Rehire Date" column exists and convert using convert_to_date
    if("Rehire Date" %in% colnames(missouri_works)){
      missouri_works$`Rehire Date`=convert_to_date(missouri_works$`Rehire Date`)
    }
    #Ensure "Termination Date" column exists and convert using convert_to_date
    if("Termination Date" %in% colnames(missouri_works)){
      missouri_works$`Termination Date`=convert_to_date(missouri_works$`Termination Date`)
    }
    missouri_works=missouri_works%>%mutate(`Termination Date`=convert_to_date(`Termination Date`),
                                           `Rehire Date`=convert_to_date(`Rehire Date`))
    missouri_works=missouri_works%>%mutate(`Termination Date`=case_when(
      !is.na(`Rehire Date`) & !is.na(`Termination Date`) & `Rehire Date`>`Termination Date`~NA_Date_,
      TRUE~`Termination Date`))
    combined_payroll_data_filtered=combined_payroll_data_filtered%>%distinct()
    build_periods=function(year,month=NULL,quarter=NULL){
      #Output list to hold all formats
      periods=list()
      if(!is.null(month)){
        #Convert month name to number if needed
        if(is.character(month)){
          current_month=match(tolower(month),tolower(month.name))
          if(is.na(current_month)) stop("Invalid month name")
        } else{
          current_month=as.integer(month)
        }
        #Current period
        periods$current_MM_YYYY=sprintf("%02d.%d",current_month,year)
        periods$current_YYYY_MM=sprintf("%d.%02d",year,current_month)
        #Previous month
        prev_month=current_month-1
        prev_year=year
        if(prev_month==0){
          prev_month=12
          prev_year=year-1
        }
        periods$prev_MM_YYYY=sprintf("%02d.%d",prev_month,prev_year)
        periods$prev_YYYY_MM=sprintf("%d.%02d",prev_year,prev_month)
        #Previous quarter
        prev_quarter=((current_month-1) %/% 3)
        if(prev_quarter==0) prev_quarter=4
        prev_q_year=ifelse(current_month<=3,year-1,year)
        periods$prev_QX_YYYY=sprintf("Q%d.%d",prev_quarter,prev_q_year)
        periods$prev_YYYY_QX=sprintf("%d.Q%d",prev_q_year,prev_quarter)
      } else if(!is.null(quarter)){
        #Current quarter periods
        periods$current_QX_YYYY=sprintf("Q%d.%d",quarter,year)
        periods$current_YYYY_QX=sprintf("%d.Q%d",year,quarter)
        #Previous quarter
        prev_quarter=quarter-1
        prev_year=year
        if(prev_quarter==0){
          prev_quarter=4
          prev_year=year-1
        }
        periods$prev_QX_YYYY=sprintf("Q%d.%d",prev_quarter,prev_year)
        periods$prev_YYYY_QX=sprintf("%d.Q%d",prev_year,prev_quarter)
        #Previous month = last month of previous quarter
        last_month_prev_q=prev_quarter*3
        periods$prev_MM_YYYY=sprintf("%02d.%d",last_month_prev_q,prev_year)
        periods$prev_YYYY_MM=sprintf("%d.%02d",prev_year,last_month_prev_q)
      }
      return(periods)
    }
    #Assuming you have year, and either month or quarter defined
    periods=build_periods(year,month=month,quarter=quarter)
    file_path=paste0(directory,"Programs/Missouri Works/",company,"/",company," Missouri Works Report.xlsx")
    report_start_row=4
    if(file.exists(file_path)){
      #List candidate previous period sheet names to try, ordered by preference
      candidate_prev_periods=c(periods$prev_MM_YYYY,periods$prev_YYYY_MM,periods$prev_QX_YYYY,periods$prev_YYYY_QX)
      #Find the first previous period sheet that exists in the workbook
      prev_period=NULL
      for(candidate in candidate_prev_periods){
        if(!is.null(candidate) && candidate %in% sheet_names){
          prev_period=candidate
          break
        }
      }
      if(!is.null(prev_period)){
        prev_report=read.xlsx(wb,sheet=prev_period,startRow=report_start_row,colNames=TRUE,detectDates=TRUE)
        #Clean column names (replace dots with spaces)
        names(prev_report)=gsub("\\."," ",names(prev_report))
        #Ensure key columns are character type
        if("Employee ID" %in% colnames(prev_report)){
          prev_report$`Employee ID`=as.character(prev_report$`Employee ID`)
        }
      }
    }
    message("Previous report imported.")
    if(file.exists(file_path)){
      #Get all sheet names
      sheets=sheet_names
      #Regex pattern to match all four formats:
      #MM.YYYY or YYYY.MM or QX.YYYY or YYYY.QX
      pattern="^((0?[1-9]|1[0-2])\\.[0-9]{4}|[0-9]{4}\\.(0[1-9]|1[0-2])|Q[1-4]\\.[0-9]{4}|[0-9]{4}\\.Q[1-4])$"
      valid_sheets=sheets[str_detect(sheets,pattern)]
      if(length(valid_sheets)>0){
        #Convert sheet names into comparable dates
        sheet_dates=sapply(valid_sheets,function(s){
          #Detect format by position of year vs month/quarter
          if(str_detect(s,"^(0?[1-9]|1[0-2])\\.[0-9]{4}$")){  
            #MM.YYYY
            parts=str_split(s,"\\.")[[1]]
            month=as.integer(trimws(parts[1]))
            year=as.integer(trimws(parts[2]))
            as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,month))
          } else if(str_detect(s,"^[0-9]{4}\\.(0[1-9]|1[0-2])$")){  
            #YYYY.MM
            parts=str_split(s,"\\.")[[1]]
            year=as.integer(trimws(parts[1]))
            month=as.integer(trimws(parts[2]))
            as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,month))
          } else if(str_detect(s,"^Q[1-4]\\.[0-9]{4}$")){  
            #QX.YYYY
            parts=str_split(s,"\\.")[[1]]
            quarter=as.integer(str_remove(trimws(parts[1]),"Q"))
            year=as.integer(trimws(parts[2]))
            month=(quarter-1)*3+1
            as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,month))
          } else if(str_detect(s,"^[0-9]{4}\\.Q[1-4]$")){  
            #YYYY.QX
            parts=str_split(s,"\\.")[[1]]
            year=as.integer(trimws(parts[1]))
            quarter=as.integer(str_remove(trimws(parts[2]),"Q"))
            month=(quarter-1)*3+1
            as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,month))
          } else{
            NA #Unexpected format
          }
        })
        #Remove any NA dates (unexpected formats)
        valid_sheets=valid_sheets[!is.na(sheet_dates)]
        sheet_dates=sheet_dates[!is.na(sheet_dates)]
        #Define current period date for comparison
        current_period_date=if(!is.null(month)){
          as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,month_num))
        } else if(!is.null(quarter)){
          as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,(quarter-1)*3+1))
        } else{
          NULL
        }
        #Keep only sheets before current period
        valid_sheets=valid_sheets[sheet_dates<current_period_date]
        sheet_dates=sheet_dates[sheet_dates<current_period_date]    
        if(length(valid_sheets)>0){
          #Pick most recent sheet prior to current period
          most_recent_sheet=valid_sheets[which.max(sheet_dates)]
          #Read prior sheet
          recent_df=read.xlsx(wb,sheet=most_recent_sheet,startRow=4,colNames=TRUE,detectDates=TRUE)
          #Clean column names (replace dots with spaces)
          names(recent_df)=gsub("\\."," ",names(recent_df))
          #Extract status columns in prior sheet
          status_cols=grep("^[A-Za-z]{3}-[0-9]{2} Status$",names(recent_df),value=TRUE)
          if(length(status_cols)>0){
            #Reorder status cols by date (most recent first)
            col_order=order(as.Date(paste0("01-",status_cols),format="%d-%b-%y"),decreasing=TRUE)
            status_cols=status_cols[col_order]
            #Move status columns to end
            existing_status_cols=intersect(status_cols,names(missouri_works))
            if(length(existing_status_cols)>0){
              missouri_works=missouri_works%>%select(-all_of(existing_status_cols),all_of(existing_status_cols))
            }
          }
        }
      }
      message("Sheets after current period removed.")
    } else{
      #File doesn't exist - continue or skip silently or add your fallback here
      message("File does not exist: ",file_path)
      #Or do nothing if you want to silently continue
    }
    #Function to get override start date and reason per employee
    get_override_start_info=function(emp_id,combined_df){
      emp_data=combined_df%>%filter(`Employee ID`==emp_id)%>%arrange(`Pay Date`)
      override_date=NA
      determination=NA
      if(is.na(override_date) && "Work State" %in% names(emp_data) && length(emp_data$`Work State`)>=2){
        ws_vec=emp_data$`Work State`
        class_vec=emp_data$Classification
        wage_vec=emp_data$`Wage Type`
        for(i in 2:length(ws_vec)){
          if(!is.na(ws_vec[i-1]) && !is.na(ws_vec[i]) && !is.na(class_vec[i-1]) && !is.na(class_vec[i])){
            #Case 1: Location only (Not MO -> MO while staying Full-Time)
            if(ws_vec[i-1]!="Missouri" && ws_vec[i]=="Missouri" && class_vec[i-1]=="Full-Time" && class_vec[i]=="Full-Time"){
              override_date=emp_data$`Pay Date`[i]-days(18)
              determination="Location"
              break
            }
            #Case 2: Classification only (Part-Time -> Full-Time while Work State stays MO)
            if(ws_vec[i-1]=="Missouri" && ws_vec[i]=="Missouri" && class_vec[i-1]=="Part-Time" && class_vec[i]=="Full-Time"){
              override_date=emp_data$`Pay Date`[i]-days(18)
              determination="Part-Time"
              break
            }
            #Case 3: Both at once (Not MO -> MO AND Part-Time -> Full-Time)
            if(ws_vec[i-1]!="Missouri" && ws_vec[i]=="Missouri" && class_vec[i-1]=="Part-Time" && class_vec[i]=="Full-Time"){
              override_date=emp_data$`Pay Date`[i]-days(18)
              determination="Both"
              break
            }
            #Case 4: Wage Type goes from Hourly -> Salaried but Classification stays Part-Time
            if(wage_vec[i-1]=="Hourly" && wage_vec[i]=="Salaried" &&
               class_vec[i-1]=="Part-Time" && class_vec[i]=="Part-Time" &&
               emp_data$`Total Hours Worked`[i]>70){
              override_date=emp_data$`Pay Date`[i]-days(18)
              determination="Part-Time - Didn't Change"
              break
            }
          }
        }
      }
      return(list(override_date=override_date,determination=determination))
    }
    #Apply the override logic and extract both values
    override_results=lapply(missouri_works$`Employee ID`,get_override_start_info,combined_df=combined_payroll_data)
    #Convert results into a data frame
    start_date_override=data.frame(EmployeeID=missouri_works$`Employee ID`,
                                    StartDate=sapply(override_results,function(x) x$override_date),
                                    Determination=sapply(override_results,function(x) x$determination),
                                    stringsAsFactors=FALSE,check.names=FALSE)
    #Define dynamic cutoff for start_date_override
    if(!is.null(month)){
      month_num=match(tolower(month),tolower(month.name))
      #Nominal bounds for the month
      period_start_nominal=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,month_num))
      period_end_nominal=seq(period_start_nominal,by="1 month",length=2)[2]-1
      #Earliest Pay Date in the month
      earliest_date=combined_payroll_data%>%filter(`Pay Date`>=period_start_nominal,`Pay Date`<=period_end_nominal)%>%
                                            summarise(min_date=min(`Pay Date`,na.rm=TRUE))%>%pull(min_date)
      if(!is.na(earliest_date)){
        start_date_override_cutoff=earliest_date-21
      } else{
        start_date_override_cutoff=period_start_nominal-21 #fallback
      }
    } else if(!is.null(quarter)){
      start_month_num=(quarter-1)*3+1
      end_month_num=quarter*3
      #Nominal bounds for the quarter
      period_start_nominal=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,start_month_num))
      last_month_date=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,end_month_num))
      period_end_nominal=seq(last_month_date,by="1 month",length=2)[2]-1  
      #Earliest Pay Date in the quarter
      earliest_date=combined_payroll_data%>%filter(`Pay Date`>=period_start_nominal,`Pay Date`<=period_end_nominal)%>%
                                            summarise(min_date=min(`Pay Date`,na.rm=TRUE))%>%pull(min_date)  
      if(!is.na(earliest_date)){
        start_date_override_cutoff=earliest_date-21
      } else{
        start_date_override_cutoff=period_start_nominal-21 #fallback
      }
    }
    #Rename to add proper spaces
    colnames(start_date_override)=c("Employee ID","Start Date","Determination")
    #Remove rows with NA Start Date
    start_date_override=start_date_override[!is.na(start_date_override$`Start Date`),]
    #Keep the earliest Start Date per employee
    start_date_override=start_date_override%>%group_by(`Employee ID`)%>%slice_min(`Start Date`,with_ties=FALSE)%>%ungroup()
    message("Start Date Override dates determined.")
    #Convert MO Works Start Date to Date (if numeric)
    if(is.numeric(missouri_works$`MO Works Start Date`)){
      missouri_works$`MO Works Start Date`=as.Date(missouri_works$`MO Works Start Date`,origin="1899-12-30")
    }
    #Convert Start Date to Date (if numeric)
    if(is.numeric(start_date_override$`Start Date`)){
      start_date_override$`Start Date`=convert_to_date(start_date_override$`Start Date`)
    }
    #Get earliest Pay Date in current period
    if(!is.null(month)){
      month_num=match(tolower(month),tolower(month.name))
      period_start=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,month_num))
      period_end=seq(period_start,by="1 month",length=2)[2]-1
    } else if(!is.null(quarter)){
      start_month_num=(quarter-1)*3+1
      end_month_num=quarter*3
      period_start=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,start_month_num))
      last_month_date=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,end_month_num))
      period_end=seq(last_month_date,by="1 month",length=2)[2]-1
    } else{
      stop("Either month or quarter must be defined")
    }
    if("Determination" %in% names(missouri_works)){
      missouri_works=missouri_works%>%select(-Determination)
    }
    #Merge start_date_override into missouri_works (including Comments logic)
    if(nrow(start_date_override)>0){
      #Build prefix label for Notes
      if(exists("month") && !is.null(month)){
        month_num=match(tolower(month),tolower(month.name))
        prefix=paste0(year,".",sprintf("%02d",month_num),": ")
      } else if(exists("quarter") && !is.null(quarter)) {
        prefix=paste0(year,".Q",quarter,": ")
      } else{
        prefix=""
      }
      #Cutoff date (earliest pay date in current period - 21)
      period_pay_dates=combined_payroll_data%>%
        filter(`Pay Date`>=period_start & `Pay Date`<=period_end)%>%pull(`Pay Date`)%>%as.Date()
      cutoff_date=if(length(period_pay_dates)>0){
        min(period_pay_dates,na.rm=TRUE)-21
      } else{
        as.Date(NA)
      }
      #Make notes table
      start_override_notes=start_date_override%>%ungroup()%>%mutate(
        Note=case_when(
          Determination=="Both"~paste0(prefix,"Switched from PT to FT and SIT state from Not MO to MO pay period starting ",format(`Start Date`,"%m/%d/%Y")),
          Determination=="Location"~paste0(prefix,"Switched SIT state from Not MO to MO pay period starting ",format(`Start Date`,"%m/%d/%Y")),
          Determination=="Part-Time"~paste0(prefix,"Switched from PT to FT pay period starting ",format(`Start Date`,"%m/%d/%Y")),
          TRUE~NA_character_),
        Note=ifelse(!is.na(`Start Date`) & !is.na(cutoff_date) & `Start Date`<cutoff_date,NA_character_,Note))%>%
      select(`Employee ID`,Note)%>%mutate(`Employee ID`=as.character(`Employee ID`))
      #Prep end override table
      start_date_override2=start_date_override%>%ungroup()%>%
        mutate(`Employee ID`=as.character(`Employee ID`))%>%
        rename(start_override_date=`Start Date`,start_determination=Determination)
      #Merge into missouri_works
      missouri_works=missouri_works%>%
        select(-any_of(c("start_override_date","start_determination")))%>%
        left_join(start_date_override2,by="Employee ID")%>%
        mutate(override_start=if_else(!is.na(start_override_date) & !is.na(start_determination) &
                                      start_determination!="Remained Salaried" & 
                                      (is.na(`MO Works Start Date`)|start_override_date>=`MO Works Start Date`),
                                      pmax(start_override_date,`Rehire Date`,`Hire Date`,na.rm=TRUE),as.Date(NA)),
                                      `MO Works Start Date`=coalesce(override_start,`MO Works Start Date`))%>%
        select(-any_of(c("override_start","start_override_date","start_determination")))%>%
        left_join(start_override_notes,by="Employee ID")%>%
        mutate(`Comments New`=ifelse(is.na(`Comments New`),"",`Comments New`), #Convert NA to ""
                `Comments New`=case_when(!is.na(Note) & `Comments New`!=""~paste0(`Comments New`,"; ",Note),
                !is.na(Note) & `Comments New`==""~Note,TRUE~`Comments New`)) #Convert back empty strings to NA
    }
    message("Start Date Override comments imported into Missouri Works.")
    #Convert all numeric-like columns to numeric while preserving NA values
    missouri_works=missouri_works%>%mutate(across(where(~all(is.na(.)|str_detect(.,"^\\d+$"))),~as.numeric(.)))
    missouri_works$`Employee ID`=as.character(missouri_works$`Employee ID`)
    #Apply convert_to_date to all columns in missouri_works that contain "Date" in the column name
    date_cols=grep("Date",colnames(missouri_works),ignore.case=TRUE,value=TRUE)
    #Loop through each matching column and apply the function
    for(col in date_cols){
      missouri_works[[col]]=convert_to_date(missouri_works[[col]])
    }
    missouri_works=missouri_works%>%select(-any_of(c("override_start_date","Note")))
    #Function to get override end date and reason per employee
    get_override_end_info=function(emp_id,combined_df){
      emp_data=combined_df%>%filter(`Employee ID`==emp_id)%>%arrange(`Pay Date`)
      override_date=NA
      determination=NA
      if(is.na(override_date) && "Work State" %in% names(emp_data) &&
         "Classification" %in% names(emp_data) && nrow(emp_data)>=2){
        ws_vec=emp_data$`Work State`
        class_vec=emp_data$Classification
        wt_vec=emp_data$`Wage Type`
        for(i in 2:length(ws_vec)){
          if(!is.na(ws_vec[i-1]) && !is.na(ws_vec[i]) && !is.na(class_vec[i-1]) && !is.na(class_vec[i])){
            #Case 1: Full-Time → Part-Time, Work State stays MO
            if(class_vec[i-1]=="Full-Time" && class_vec[i]=="Part-Time" && 
               ws_vec[i-1]=="Missouri" && ws_vec[i]=="Missouri" && wt_vec[i]!="Salaried"){
              override_date=emp_data$`Pay Date`[i]-days(21)
              determination="Part-Time"
              break
            }
            #Case 2: MO → Not MO, Classification stays Full-Time
            if(ws_vec[i-1]=="Missouri" && ws_vec[i]!="Missouri" && class_vec[i-1]=="Full-Time" && class_vec[i]=="Full-Time"){
              override_date=emp_data$`Pay Date`[i]-days(21)
              determination="Location"
              break
            }
            #Case 3: MO → Not MO *and* Full-Time → Part-Time
            if(ws_vec[i-1]=="Missouri" && ws_vec[i]!="Missouri" && class_vec[i-1]=="Full-Time" && class_vec[i]=="Part-Time"){
              override_date=emp_data$`Pay Date`[i]-days(21)
              determination="Both"
              break
            }
            #Case 4: Full-Time → Part-Time, Work State stays MO, Wage Type stayed Salaried
            if(class_vec[i-1]=="Full-Time" && class_vec[i]=="Part-Time" && ws_vec[i-1]=="Missouri" && ws_vec[i]=="Missouri" &&
               wt_vec[i-1]=="Salaried" && wt_vec[i]=="Salaried"){
              override_date=emp_data$`Pay Date`[i]-days(21)
              determination="Remained Salaried"
              break
            }
          }
        }
      }
      return(list(override_date=override_date,determination=determination))
    }
    #Apply the override logic and extract both values
    override_results=lapply(missouri_works$`Employee ID`,get_override_end_info,combined_df=combined_payroll_data)
    #Convert results into a data frame
    end_date_override=data.frame(EmployeeID=missouri_works$`Employee ID`,
                                 EndDate=sapply(override_results,function(x) x$override_date),
                                 Determination=sapply(override_results,function(x) x$determination),
                                 stringsAsFactors=FALSE,check.names=FALSE)
    #Define dynamic cutoff for end_date_override
    if(!is.null(month)){
      month_num=match(tolower(month),tolower(month.name))
      #Nominal bounds for the month
      period_start_nominal=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,month_num))
      period_end_nominal=seq(period_start_nominal,by="1 month",length=2)[2]-1
      #Earliest Pay Date in the month
      earliest_date=combined_payroll_data%>%filter(`Pay Date`>=period_start_nominal,`Pay Date`<=period_end_nominal)%>%
                                            summarise(min_date=min(`Pay Date`,na.rm=TRUE))%>%pull(min_date)
      if(!is.na(earliest_date)){
        end_date_override_cutoff=earliest_date-21
      } else{
        end_date_override_cutoff=period_start_nominal-21 #fallback
      }
    } else if(!is.null(quarter)){
      start_month_num=(quarter-1)*3+1
      end_month_num=quarter*3
      #Nominal bounds for the quarter
      period_start_nominal=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,start_month_num))
      last_month_date=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,end_month_num))
      period_end_nominal=seq(last_month_date,by="1 month",length=2)[2]-1  
      #Earliest Pay Date in the quarter
      earliest_date=combined_payroll_data%>%filter(`Pay Date`>=period_start_nominal,`Pay Date`<=period_end_nominal)%>%
                                            summarise(min_date=min(`Pay Date`,na.rm=TRUE))%>%pull(min_date)  
      if(!is.na(earliest_date)){
        end_date_override_cutoff=earliest_date-21
      } else{
        end_date_override_cutoff=period_start_nominal-21 #fallback
      }
    }
    #Rename to add proper spaces
    colnames(end_date_override)=c("Employee ID","End Date","Determination")
    #Remove rows with NA End Date
    end_date_override=end_date_override[!is.na(end_date_override$`End Date`),]
    #Keep the earliest End Date per employee
    end_date_override=end_date_override%>%group_by(`Employee ID`)%>%slice_min(`End Date`,with_ties=FALSE)%>%ungroup()
    message("End Date Override dates determined.")
    #Convert MO Works End Date to Date (if numeric)
    if(is.numeric(missouri_works$`MO Works End Date`)){
      missouri_works$`MO Works End Date`=as.Date(missouri_works$`MO Works End Date`,origin="1899-12-30")
    }
    #Convert End Date to Date (if numeric)
    if(is.numeric(end_date_override$`End Date`)){
      end_date_override$`End Date`=convert_to_date(end_date_override$`End Date`)
    }
    #Get earliest Pay Date in current period
    if(!is.null(month)){
      month_num=match(tolower(month),tolower(month.name))
      period_start=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,month_num))
      period_end=seq(period_start,by="1 month",length=2)[2]-1
    } else if(!is.null(quarter)){
      start_month_num=(quarter-1)*3+1
      end_month_num=quarter*3
      period_start=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,start_month_num))
      last_month_date=as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-01",year,end_month_num))
      period_end=seq(last_month_date,by="1 month",length=2)[2]-1
    } else{
      stop("Either month or quarter must be defined")
    }
    if("Determination" %in% names(missouri_works)){
      missouri_works=missouri_works%>%select(-Determination)
    }
    #Merge end_date_override into missouri_works (including Comments logic)
    if(nrow(end_date_override)>0){
      #Build prefix label for Notes
      if(exists("month") && !is.null(month)){
        month_num=match(tolower(month),tolower(month.name))
        prefix=paste0(year,".",sprintf("%02d",month_num),": ")
      } else if(exists("quarter") && !is.null(quarter)) {
        prefix=paste0(year,".Q",quarter,": ")
      } else{
        prefix=""
      }
      #Cutoff date (earliest pay date in current period - 21)
      period_pay_dates=combined_payroll_data%>%
        filter(`Pay Date`>=period_start & `Pay Date`<=period_end)%>%pull(`Pay Date`)%>%as.Date()
      cutoff_date=if(length(period_pay_dates)>0){
        min(period_pay_dates,na.rm=TRUE)-21
      } else{
        as.Date(NA)
      }
      #Make notes table
      end_override_notes=end_date_override%>%ungroup()%>%mutate(
        Note=case_when(
          Determination=="Both"~paste0(prefix,"Switched from FT to PT and SIT state from MO to Not MO pay period ending ",format(`End Date`,"%m/%d/%Y")),
          Determination=="Location"~paste0(prefix,"Switched SIT state from MO to Not MO pay period ending ",format(`End Date`,"%m/%d/%Y")),
          Determination=="Part-Time"~paste0(prefix,"Switched from FT to PT pay period ending ",format(`End Date`,"%m/%d/%Y")),
          Determination=="Remained Salaried"~paste0(prefix,"Switched from FT to PT but remained salaried pay period ending ",format(`End Date`,"%m/%d/%Y")),
          TRUE~NA_character_),
        Note=ifelse(!is.na(`End Date`) & !is.na(cutoff_date) & `End Date`<cutoff_date,NA_character_,Note))%>%
      select(`Employee ID`,Note)%>%mutate(`Employee ID`=as.character(`Employee ID`))
      #Prep end override table
      end_date_override2=end_date_override%>%ungroup()%>%
        mutate(`Employee ID`=as.character(`Employee ID`))%>%
        rename(end_override_date=`End Date`,end_determination=Determination)
      #Merge into missouri_works
      missouri_works=missouri_works%>%
        select(-any_of(c("end_override_date","end_determination")))%>%
        left_join(end_date_override2,by="Employee ID")%>%
        mutate(override_end=if_else(!is.na(end_override_date) & !is.na(end_determination) &
                                    end_determination!="Remained Salaried" & 
                                    (is.na(`MO Works Start Date`)|end_override_date>=`MO Works Start Date`),
                                    pmin(end_override_date,`Termination Date`,na.rm=TRUE),as.Date(NA)),
                                    `MO Works End Date`=coalesce(override_end,`MO Works End Date`))%>%
        select(-any_of(c("override_end","end_override_date","end_determination")))%>%
        left_join(end_override_notes,by="Employee ID")%>%
        mutate(`Comments New`=ifelse(is.na(`Comments New`),"",`Comments New`), #Convert NA to ""
                `Comments New`=case_when(!is.na(Note) & `Comments New`!=""~paste0(`Comments New`,"; ",Note),
                !is.na(Note) & `Comments New`==""~Note,TRUE~`Comments New`)) #Convert back empty strings to NA
    }
    message("End Date Override comments imported into Missouri Works.")
    #Convert all numeric-like columns to numeric while preserving NA values
    missouri_works=missouri_works%>%mutate(across(where(~all(is.na(.)|str_detect(.,"^\\d+$"))),~as.numeric(.)))
    missouri_works$`Employee ID`=as.character(missouri_works$`Employee ID`)
    #Apply convert_to_date to all columns in missouri_works that contain "Date" in the column name
    date_cols=grep("Date",colnames(missouri_works),ignore.case=TRUE,value=TRUE)
    #Loop through each matching column and apply the function
    for(col in date_cols){
      missouri_works[[col]]=convert_to_date(missouri_works[[col]])
    }
    message("Date columns aligned.")
    missouri_works=missouri_works%>%select(-any_of(c("Note")))
    #Define file path and sheet
